title: The US has lost the reputation war — to Russia and China
content: A new global survey reveals a significant drop in America's international image under President Trump, now trailing behind Russia and China.  Concerns over his foreign policy, tariffs, and military actions have unsettled allies and created global unease.  This decline marks the second consecutive year of falling perceptions for the US.

title: Mulayam Singh Yadav's son Prateek poisoned? SP leader makes shock claim
content: Samajwadi Party leader Ravidas Mehrotra alleged that Prateek Yadav's death was not normal, suggesting poison as a possible cause due to reported bruises and his healthy lifestyle. Prateek Yadav, 38, passed away in Lucknow, with doctors declaring him brought dead at the hospital. His death has prompted condolences from political leaders across party lines.

title: Hardik Pandya Update: After over 3 hrs training, will he play PBKS match?
content: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya's intense training session fuels speculation amid his absence due to back spasms. While the team cites recovery, his vigorous workout raises questions about his availability. Pandya and stand-in skipper Suryakumar Yadav are currently away from the squad, impacting the team's forgettable season where they have already been eliminated from playoff race.

title: 'Not a big deal': Rajasthan minister Madan Dilawar downplays NEET-UG 2026 exam cancellation
content: Rajasthan's Education Minister downplayed the NEET-UG 2026 cancellation, calling it "not a big issue" if irregularities occurred. However, Congress leaders accused the state government of suppressing information about the alleged paper leak, which involved a multi-state network and a "guess paper" sold for lakhs.

title: China trip: Why Trump invited Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at the 11th hour
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was added to President Trump's China visit delegation at the last minute, reportedly at Trump's personal request. Huang's presence was seen as a political liability due to ongoing US-China tensions over advanced chip sales to China, a market where Nvidia has lost significant share.

title: Rupee at 95.74, hits all-time low as oil prices pressure economy
content: The Indian rupee hit a new low against the US dollar, pressured by rising oil prices and external debt. Analysts link the decline to the US-Iran conflict, which has boosted crude oil costs and strained India's economy. While the RBI and government are intervening, markets anticipate potential interest rate hikes to stabilize the currency and manage inflation.

title: Prabhsimran Singh hits back with 'shushing' emoji story amid PBKS social media criticism
content: Punjab Kings opener Prabhsimran Singh has responded to online criticism regarding his form and fitness, echoing co-owner Preity Zinta's stance against misinformation. Despite recent struggles, Singh remains a key scorer for PBKS, who are facing a crucial must-win match against Mumbai Indians after a four-game losing streak.

title: Microsoft Israel has been placed under the management of Microsoft France, here's why
content: Microsoft Israel's chief has resigned following an internal probe into the misuse of Azure cloud services by the Israeli Ministry of Defense.  The investigation revealed ethical breaches and a lack of transparency, impacting Microsoft's global standing.  This comes after earlier revelations about Unit 8200's surveillance activities, complicating future contract renewals.

title: Kuki church leaders ambushed in Manipur: 3 clergy members killed en route to peace meeting
content: Three Kuki church leaders were killed and four injured in an armed ambush in Manipur's Kangpokpi district while traveling to an inter-association ecclesiastical meeting. Community sources and the Kuki Organisation for Human Rights Trust alleged involvement of NSCN-IM and ZUF, calling it a premeditated attack targeting peace efforts.

title: Months after divorce posts, Prateek and Aparna Yadav shared pics together of vacation in Iceland
content: Weeks after a public marital dispute, Prateek Yadav and BJP leader Aparna Yadav vacationed in Iceland with their daughter, sharing photos of a seemingly resolved conflict. The trip followed Prateek's accusations of Aparna prioritizing fame and his subsequent announcement of reconciliation, just months before his sudden death.

title: ED tightens grip on Nashik astrologer ‘Bhondu Baba’, summons NCP leader Rupali Chakankar
content: The ED has directed Chakankar to appear before the agency for questioning later this week as part of its ongoing probe into the matter. Previously, the ED had also questioned Chakankar's sister in connection with the same case.

title: Musk, Pichai are reportedly in talks for data centers that Sam Altman publicly made fun of
content: Google is reportedly in talks with SpaceX for a deal to launch orbital data centers into space, aiming to address energy demands of traditional facilities. While Google and SpaceX see potential in this unproven technology, OpenAI's Sam Altman deems it 'ridiculous' for this decade. Google plans prototype satellite launches by 2027.

title: 'Big plus for RCB': Mohammad Kaif highlights major Varun Chakaravarthy concern for KKR
content: Former India batter Mohammad Kaif believes spinner Varun Chakaravarthy's potential absence against RCB would be a significant boost for Bengaluru. Chakaravarthy's recent return to form has been crucial for KKR's winning streak, but an injury concern looms. Kaif highlighted Virat Kohli's comfort against faster spinners, suggesting his absence would benefit RCB.

title: Russia tests ‘world’s most powerful missile’: How RS-28 Sarmat ‘Satan II’ works
content: Russia has successfully tested its new RS-28 Sarmat intercontinental ballistic missile, dubbed 'Satan II' by NATO. President Putin hailed it as the world's most powerful nuclear weapon, capable of carrying multiple warheads and evading missile defenses.

title: Bengal CM Suvendu retains Bhabanipur, seat where he defeated Mamata, over Nandigram
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari has chosen to keep the Bhabanipur seat and will vacate Nandigram, despite winning both in the recent assembly elections. Adhikari, who previously defeated Mamata Banerjee in Nandigram, had challenged her again by contesting from Bhabanipur. He stated his inability to hold MLA positions from two constituencies and has taken his oath from Bhabanipur.

title: Vijay clears floor test with 144 MLAs backing TVK: How the high-stakes assembly showdown unfolded
content: Tamil Nadu CM Joseph Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) successfully passed a crucial floor test, securing the support of several parties including a rebel AIADMK faction. This marks the first coalition government led by a new entrant, breaking the decades-long Dravidian political dominance. Vijay's victory solidifies his position as the state's first non-DMK, non-AIADMK chief minister since 1967.

title: NIA arrests narco-terror accused Iqbal Singh alias Shera after extradition from Portugal
content: National Investigation Agency has arrested Iqbal Singh alias Shera. He is a wanted narco-terrorist. His extradition from Portugal followed diplomatic and legal efforts. Shera is considered the mastermind in a Hizb-ul-Mujahideen terror financing case. His arrest is a significant breakthrough in tackling Pakistan-backed narco-terror networks.

title: ‘Unfair trade practice’: EPFO pulled up over PF delay, awards Rs 50,000 compensation
content: Chandigarh Consumer Commission penalised EPFO Rs 50,000 for a decade-long delay in transferring provident fund. Software glitches were dismissed as an excuse for the significant lapse. The commission found EPFO guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practice, ordering compensation for the employee's prolonged wait for his PF funds.

title: GT’s 2nd-half IPL surge explained: Holder gamble, bowling brutality, and?
content: Gujarat Titans, after a slow start in IPL 2026, are on the cusp of playoff qualification with eight wins from twelve matches. Their resurgence is fueled by a dominant opening pair, the impactful inclusion of Jason Holder, and a lethal bowling attack that has stifled opposition batting.

title: Do India’s forex reserves cover enough to defend rupee? Why economists are confident
content: Economists believe India’s foreign-exchange reserves are still sufficiently strong to shield the rupee from the oil price shock triggered by the Iran conflict, with the country’s reserve buffers remaining far healthier than the levels seen during the 2013 taper tantrum.

title: CBSE class 12 pass percentage stands at 85.20%; girls outperform boys
content: CBSE declared the Class XII board examination results for the 2025-26 academic session with an overall pass percentage of 85.20%. Out of 17.69 lakh students who appeared, 15.07 lakh cleared the examination. Girls recorded a higher pass percentage than boys, while transgender candidates achieved a 100% pass rate. More than 94,000 students scored above 90%. CBSE also introduced On Screen Marking for digital evaluation and announced supplementary examinations from July 15, 2026.

title: Foreign outflow worries ‘overstated’: S&P confident about India amid Middle East crisis
content: S&P Global has said that the country has adequate financial buffers to manage a wider current account deficit that could emerge because of rising crude oil prices. India’s current account deficit has narrowed over the past few years, although fluctuations in global crude prices now pose a risk to that improvement.

title: Even Mahabharat would pale: Supreme Court on Kapur family property dispute
content: A protracted property dispute between Sunjay Kapur's mother and wife has prompted the Supreme Court to appoint former CJI D Y Chandrachud as mediator. The apex court noted the legal battle's intensity, comparing it to the Mahabharat. A fresh plea alleges circumvention of a previous order, with the court set to hear the matter further.

title: Ex-IPL chief Shukla slams ultra-aggressive batting; Vettori calls it off-day
content: Former IPL chairman Rajeev Shukla questioned Sunrisers Hyderabad's aggressive batting approach after their crushing defeat to Gujarat Titans. Despite a chaseable target of 169, SRH collapsed spectacularly, losing wickets early while attempting big shots. Head coach Daniel Vettori defended the team's philosophy, calling it a rare off-day.

title: ‘Removed uterus left in box’: 4 women die after C-section surgery in Kota govt hosp; 6 critical
content: Another woman has died from post-cesarean section complications at Kota's New Medical College Hospital, marking the fourth fatality in a week. Pinki Mahawar, 30, succumbed after being transferred from JK Lon Hospital, with her husband alleging a delayed referral. The incident has led to protests and calls for accountability regarding safety protocols in government hospitals.

title: IPL Playoff Scenarios: GT surge to 99.6%, where do other teams stand?
content: With 14 games left, Gujarat Titans are almost guaranteed a top-four finish with a 99.6% chance, while RCB also has a strong 88.1% probability. Sunrisers Hyderabad and Punjab Kings' playoff hopes are still alive but diminished. Chennai Super Kings and Rajasthan Royals hold a slight edge for the top four, with Kolkata Knight Riders and Delhi Capitals facing slim chances.

title: 'If Bangladesh, Nepal youth can ... ': Arvind Kejriwal invokes Gen Z after NEET paper leak
content: Arvind Kejriwal urged India's Gen Z to demand accountability in the alleged NEET paper leak, citing their power to drive political change. He questioned the CBI's effectiveness in past leak investigations, claiming the current probe would yield no meaningful results. Kejriwal highlighted how repeated leaks since 2014 have impacted millions of students, with many occurring in BJP-governed states.

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay revokes astrologer's appointment as OSD after allies raise objections
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay has revoked the appointment of his astrologer, Radhan Pandit, as Officer on Special Duty (Political) following opposition criticism. The decision came a day after the appointment, which had drawn objections that personal beliefs should not influence governance. Pandit, a close aide and party spokesperson, was to assist in political coordination and advisory functions.

title: Gold gets costlier: Why govt raised import duty and what changes for buyers
content: India has significantly increased import duties on gold and silver to 15% from 6%, aiming to curb rising imports and conserve foreign exchange amid the West Asia crisis. This move follows Prime Minister Modi's appeal for austerity and aims to ease pressure on the rupee and the country's balance of payments.

title: Prateek Yadav death case: UP police at BJP leader Aparna Yadav's home, seal husband's room
content: Police are investigating the death of Prateek Yadav, brother of Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav, after he was found unconscious at a Lucknow residence. Investigators, including DCP Central Zone Vikrant Vir, visited BJP leader Aparna Yadav's home and seized Prateek's mobile phone. The exact cause of death remains undetermined pending post-mortem and forensic reports.

title: Bengal chief secretary orders crackdown on illegal toll booths in Bengal
content: West Bengal's new BJP government has launched a swift crackdown on illegal toll and drop gates across the state. Chief Secretary Manoj Kumar Agarwal issued the directive, ordering immediate closure of all unauthorized collection points. District administrations are tasked with identifying and removing these illegal operations, with a report due by May 15.

title: Wanted drug lord Prabhdeep Singh extradited to India from Azerbaijan
content: India has successfully extradited fugitive Prabhdeep Singh from Azerbaijan, a key figure in a major international drug investigation. Singh, wanted under an Interpol Red Notice, was brought back by a Delhi Police team. This repatriation, a result of multi-agency efforts, marks a significant win against organized crime and adds to India's growing success in bringing wanted criminals back home.

title: Congress leader Sonia Gandhi discharged from Gurgaon's Medanta Hospital
content: Senior Congress leader Sonia Gandhi has been admitted to Medanta Hospital in Gurgaon for a minor procedure. Gandhi, a prominent figure in Indian politics and the widow of former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi, has a long and influential history within the Congress party.

title: N Rangasamy takes oath as Puducherry CM for 5th time
content: N Rangasamy has been sworn in as Puducherry's Chief Minister for a fifth term, leading an AINRC-BJP NDA government. He secured victory in two constituencies during the recent assembly elections. The NDA coalition holds a comfortable majority in the 30-member assembly, ensuring a stable government for the Union Territory.

title: Gold price prediction after duty hike: Will price rally of today sustain in coming days?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold and silver prices are rallying in the domestic market due to the sudden hike in import duties. In the coming days they are expected to align closer to the international prices, says Vedika Narvekar, Research Analyst - Commodities & Currencies, Anand Rathi Shares and Stock Brokers.

title: Report: West Asia war not hurting India inflation yet; flags fuel and crop risks ahead
content: India's retail inflation remains largely unaffected by West Asian conflict, with SBI retaining its FY27 forecast at 4.5%. While imported inflation eased slightly, rising food prices, particularly tomatoes, are a key driver. Future trends hinge on energy price pass-through and summer crop progress, with the RBI flagging upside risks.

title: 'Losses in business can break person': What Akhilesh Yadav said on stepbrother's death
content: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav expressed deep grief over the death of his younger brother, Prateek Yadav, describing him as a hardworking and health-conscious individual. Akhilesh highlighted Prateek's determination to progress through his own efforts and mentioned that financial setbacks can deeply affect individuals. The family will proceed with legal formalities and future decisions collectively.

title: Rs 12 lakh! Cummins fined after GT loss, joins Iyer, Pandya in unwanted list
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins faces a Rs 12 lakh fine. This penalty is for a slow over rate during their IPL 2026 match against Gujarat Titans. The team suffered a significant defeat. This marks the team's first such offense this season. Several other captains have also been fined for similar violations.

title: Air India cuts flights as Iran war drives up costs and hits demand
content: Air India has significantly reduced its flight schedule, cancelling around 200 weekly services from June to August due to soaring jet fuel prices and a depreciating rupee. These cuts follow previous reductions and aim to combat substantial losses. The airline is also exploring cost-saving measures like making meals optional on certain flights and unbundling lounge access for business class passengers.

title: SC asks Centre to consider States’ pleas for funds for CCTVs in police stations
content: The top court had initiated a suo motu proceeding over non-functional CCTV cameras in police stations linked to a spike in custodial deaths in recent months

title: AIADMK legislature party splits vertically during trust vote in T.N. Assembly; 25 voted in favour of TVK govt., 22 voted against
content: When Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar called for a division vote, the 47 AIADMK MLAs were clearly divided in their positions on the motion

title: NIA arrests Hizb-ul-Mujahideen linked ‘narco-terrorist’ after extradition from Portugal
content: According to the NIA, Shera was a key conspirator and handler of an India-based narco-terror module engaged in smuggling heroin from Pakistan

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly session LIVE: Chief Minister Vijay's TVK wins trust vote with 144 ‘ayes’
content: Udhayanidhi led DMK MLAs' walkout prior to voting; 25 rebel AIADMK MLAs from C.Ve. Shanmugam camp extended support to TVK while 22 AIADMK MLAs of the Edappadi Palaniswami faction voted against the confidence motion.

title: Visakhapatnam gets ₹1,501.03 crore Centre nod for water, drainage projects; Madhurawada gets lion’s share
content: Funding under Urban Challenge Fund includes a 25% GoI grant of ₹375.26 crore, with multilateral financing from IFC and loans from Canara Bank and IOB

title: Madras High Court stays Tamil Nadu police’s notice to block 18 X posts critical of TVK
content: Justices L. Victoria Gowri and N. Senthilkumar grant the interim order pursuant to a PIL petition filed by a VHP office-bearer

title: Cyber Crime Wing notice for blocking social media posts draws criticism
content: Incidentally, most of the flagged URLs/posts allegedly contained content critical of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam and Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay

title: Pinarayi Vijayan calls for scrapping NTA, demands time-bound probe into NEET leak allegations
content: He called for handing back the responsibility for conducting examinations to the respective State government agencies as was done earlier

title: CBSE Class 12 results declared, pass percentage drops to 85.20%
content: With a pass percentage of 88.86%, girls outshone boys, who had a pass percentage of 82.13%, by 6.73% in the CBSE Class 12 results

title: Andhra Pradesh: New system for vehicle registration in 24 hours; auto-approved if officials delay
content: Transport Minister M. Ramprasad Reddy said the registration would be auto-approved if officials failed to respond within 24 hours

title: ATS raids over 40 places in Maharashtra, questions 53 suspects linked to Pakistan-based gangster
content: The raids were being conducted since morning in Mumbai, Akola, Nanded, Pune, Mira Road (in Thane district), Nallasopara (Palghar district), Nashik, Jalgaon, Nagpur and various other places in the State, the official said

title: Konthuruthy river puramboke eviction: families withdraw case; settle for compensation
content: In an interim order, the High Court had directed eviction while entrusting the Kochi Corporation and the Revenue Department to finalise and implement rehabilitation measures within four months

title: Assam feels safer with BJP in power in West Bengal: Himanta Biswa Sarma
content: A day after taking oath as the Chief Minister for the second straight term, he says the political alignment between the two neighbouring States would improve coordination to face shared challenges

title: Iran war shows military force alone cannot be solution to conflicts: T.S. Tirumurti
content: The former Ambassador and Permanent Representative of India to the United Nations was speaking at the inauguration of Diplomacy and Sustainability Dialogues 2026, jointly organised by Saveetha Institute of Medical and Technical Sciences (SIMATS) and The Hindu

title: India should have stepped into vacuum left by U.S. as guarantor of security in West Asia: Talmiz Ahmad
content: Speaking on the topic ‘India’s Diplomatic Balancing in West Asia: Strategic Autonomy, Energy Security and Global Responsibility’, the former Ambassador remarked that “India is not a serious role-player as far as West Asia and the crisis over the last two and a half years is concerned.”

title: Bengal CM Adhikari to retain Bhabanipur Assembly segment, give up Nandigram
content: Mr. Adhikari defeated TMC supremo Mamata Banerjee in her Bhabanipur bastion in the recently concluded Assembly polls by a margin of 15,105 in a teeterboard battle

title: Vijay’s government revokes order appointing astrologer Rickey Radhan Pandit as OSD (Political) to CM
content: The government on Tuesday appointed him to the post and it drew severe criticism, even from TVK allies

title: AINRC chief N. Rangasamy sworn in as Chief Minister of Puducherry
content: Along with Mr. Rangasamy, Bharatiya Janata Party’s legislature party leader A. Namassivayam and AINRC legislator Malladi Krishna Rao were sworn in as Ministers in the second NDA government in the UT

title: Bandi Sanjay’s son misses time to appear before investigators in POCSO case, women stage protest at police station
content: Cyberabad police served Bandi Sai Bhagirath a notice directing him to appear before investigators at Petbasheerabad police station by 2 p.m. on Wednesday

title: Karnataka new excise regime: Revised liquor rates still being worked out
content: According to excise officials, the entry level low price-high alcohol content drinks have seen an increase in the range of ₹10 to ₹20 for 180 ml.

title: Marijuana worth ₹4 crore, concealed in trolley bag seized at Delhi airport; two held
content: Custom officials said one person was also apprehended during the operation, who had come to receive the contraband

title: Haryana mayoral polls: BJP wins 2 of 3 corporations, ahead in third; student Independent stuns party in Uklana
content: The BJP also took the president’s post in Rewari municipal council and in Dharuhera and Sampla municipal committees

title: Sub-registrars suspended after dead man signs not one but two land deals in Belagavi district
content: Two land deals, worth crores of rupees, were registered in the name of Digambar Shripad Kulkarni who had passed away on April 9, 1987

title: ‘Savukku’ Shankar gets bail in attempt-to-murder case
content: A habeas corpus petition has also been filed in the Madras High Court challenging his preventive detention under the Goondas Act

title: Srisailam temple revenue up to ₹217 crore; Anam Reddy credits CM Naidu’s tourism push
content: Footfall at the Srisailam temple rose from 82.18 lakh in 2023-24 to 119.71 lakh in 2025-26, the minister said

title: Kerala HC seeks report on siblings approaching Collector to end parents’ feud
content: The children had earlier approached the child welfare committee, citing that they were emotionally exhausted due to the feud between their parents and police cases

title: K. Vijayanand directs AP-Transco to expedite 68 power transmission projects worth ₹5,000 crore
content: On completion, the projects will add 15,274 MVA capacity and 1,822 circuit km of transmission line network

title: Trainer aircraft of private company crash lands in Baramati; no casualty reported
content: “The aircraft was flying at a comparatively low altitude when it developed a technical malfunction, and one one side of the aircraft reportedly hit a light pole before it struck the ground,” Superintendent of Police, Pune, said

title: At least 1,000 more rooms to be made available to devotees at Kukke Subrahmanya in two years
content: Cabinet clears ₹604.65 crore worth Master Plan Phase III development work, including Sarpa Samskara Yaga Shales, Ashlehsa Bali Mantapa, Anna Dasoha Bhavana and staff quarters

title: CBSE results: Tamil Nadu registers 98.48% pass rate in Class 12, 99.86% in Class 10
content: The CBSE Class 12 and Class 10 results were announced on Tuesday (May 13, 2025), with the national pass percentage recorded at 88.39% and 93.6%, respectively

title: Plea in Supreme Court to replace or restructure NTA after NEET-UG paper leak
content: “Direct the reconduct of NEET-UG 2026 under the strict scrutiny of a judicially appointed high-powered committee until an interim oversight committee constituted by the apex court has verified and certified the security of the revised examination process,” the petition sought.

title: Tamil Nadu’s political transition, growth story faultlines
content: The State’s development model is under pressure from changing realities, which the new Chief Minister needs to address

title: Supreme Court stays Madras High Court order on TVK MLA Sethupathi, terms it ‘atrocious’
content: Justice Nath said the court did not care for or differentiate between one political party or another, be it “DMK, AIADMK, TVK or BJP”

title: UDF’s near three-fourths majority marked a historic victory for the Congress-led coalition
content: Haemorrhaging vote shares across regions, the CPI(M)-led LDF registers its lowest vote share tally since 1982; Congress and IUML led the UDF surge with the former winning 63 seats, and the latter securing 22.

title: A society at large under question: rebuild a culture where safety of children is collective responsibility
content: The sexual assault and murder of a minor girl in Maharashtra has once again exposed the disturbing vulnerabilities that continue to confront children in our society

title: Six died, 12 injured due to strong winds in Sangli
content: The incident occurred on May 11 in the evening, around 5 p.m., as every Tuesday and Friday, a huge yatra is held in the Margubai temple area in Motewadi village in Jat

title: Congress will succumb to Jamaat’s demands, says Kerala BJP leader S. Suresh
content: BJP State general secretary says the Congress' failure to choose a CM candidate even nine days after the United Democratic Front emerged victorious in the Assembly elections is an insult to all Malayalis

title: Entrust Devaraja Market and Lansdowne Building restoration to heritage experts: Pramoda Devi Wadiyar
content: Her offer, made some time ago, to restore a portion of the Devaraja Market to prove that the structure could indeed be restored using traditional materials and methods was not accepted

title: PM austerity appeal: State leaders take steps to limit their fuel use
content: Top Ministers from Uttar Pradesh, Delhi, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan and Bihar, in addition to numerous BJP leaders, were among those who announced measures to reduce convoy sizes and fuel consumption

title: OPT fraud: 10,000 foreign students, including Indians, under U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement scanner
content: “Our nation will not tolerate security threats originating from the foreign student programme,” says ICE’s acting director Todd Lyons

title: What you need to know about Karnataka’s Rohith Vemula Bill
content: The revised Bill aims to prevent exclusion and injustice while safeguarding the right to education, and the dignity of students, teaching faculty, and non-teaching staff belonging to scheduled castes and scheduled tribes

title: U.S. to impose visa curbs on 13 people linked to Indian firm over fentanyl
content: KS International Traders generated revenue through trafficking in fentanyl, which President Donald Trump ​has designated as a “weapon ‌of mass destruction”, the State Department said

title: What you need to know about Karnataka’s Rohith Vemula Bill
content: The revised Bill aims to prevent exclusion and injustice while safeguarding the right to education and the dignity of students.

title: Government doubles import duty on gold & silver, effective rate now at 18.4%
content: Industry players and experts warn that this decision will not only encourage a shift to smuggling but will also have other negative effects on employment. The government has not yet released an official statement on the duty hikes

title: Koppal Mango Fair opens, offers wide variety of the fruit and related products
content: Officials said Koppal district has emerged as an important mango-growing region, with mango cultivation spread across more than 6,500 hectares. Kesar mango, in particular, has gained popularity among growers and consumers

title: Prateek Yadav's death: Condolences pour in from across party lines for Mulayam's son
content: SP leader and Prateek's uncle Shivpal Singh Yadav described the death as "extremely sad" and prayed for peace to the departed soul

title: 'Illegal' structures razed at properties of AIMIM leader accused of sheltering TCS case accused
content: Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar Municipal Corporation issued a notice to Mr. Patel over alleged illegal constructions in the city's Naregaon area, seeking a clarification from him within 72 hours. If no reply is received from Mr. Patel, the corporation has the authority to decide the property's fate, says notice

title: Police detain volunteers protesting tree felling near Hyderabad’s KBR National Park
content: Residents and environmental activists have risen up against the tree felling — around the National Park — taken up to make way for seven flyovers and seven underpasses as part of the Hyderabad City Innovative

title: INCOIS expands coastal flood monitoring to tackle ‘Kallakkadal’ surges
content: The sudden and powerful swell surges pose a significant threat to fishing communities and coastal infrastructure

title: Four cubs born to country’s 1st India-born cheetah found dead in Kuno
content: four cubs born to the country's first wild-breeding cheetah were found dead near their den site in Kuno National Park in sheopur district on Tuesday . the cub was found partially consumed, by a monitoring team around 6.30 am during routine surveillance . forest officials suspect the cubes may have fallen prey to another wild animal .

title: Suvendu Adhikari Keeps Bhabanipur Seat, Vacates Nandigram After Winning Both
content: He defeated TMC supremo Mamata Banerjee in her Bhabanipur bastion in the recently concluded Assembly polls by a margin of 15,105

title: Secret Son, Rebellious Wife: When Prateek Yadav Was At Centre Of Family Feuds
content: A division within the Samajwadi Party made Prateek Yadav a part of a larger power struggle between the SP chief's brother Shivpal Yadav and Akhilesh Yadav.

title: Secret Son, Rebellious Wife: When Prateek Yadav Was At Centre Of Family Feuds
content: A division within the Samajwadi Party made Prateek Yadav a part of a larger power struggle between the SP chief's brother Shivpal Yadav and Akhilesh Yadav.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Snakes And Ladders: How NEET Has Now Become A Trauma Machine For 22 Lakh Students
content: When incompetence becomes normalised within systems meant to shape futures, the damage extends far beyond rankings and admissions. It reshapes an entire generation's relationship with trust, effort, and hope itself.

title: Anti-Terror Agency Arrests Narco-Terrorist In Delhi After Extradition From Portugal
content: New Delhi, May 13 (IANS) The National Investigation Agency (NIA) on Wednesday arrested wanted narco-terrorist Iqbal Singh alias Shera after successfully securing his extradition from Portugal following prolonged diplomatic and legal efforts, official

title: No Relief For Ajit Pawar's Son's Firm In Rs 21 Crore Pune Land Deal Case
content: The deal came under scanner after it came to light that while executing the sale deed, a stamp duty of Rs 21 crore was waived.

title: NDTV Exclusive: How Mediator Pakistan Has Been Harbouring Iranian Military Jets For Years
content: In a statement, the government of Pakistan, while not denying the presence of the aircraft, has stated that it 'categorically rejects the CBS News report regarding the presence of Iranian aircraft at Nur Khan Air Base as misleading and sensationalise

title: N Rangasamy Sworn In As Puducherry Chief Minister For Record 5th Term
content: Puducherry, May 13 (IANS) The National Democratic Alliance (NDA) government headed by N. Rangasamy assumed office in Puducherry on Wednesday, with the veteran leader taking oath as Chief Minister for a record fifth term.

title: Woman's Career Ambition Not Cruelty Or Desertion: Top Court In Divorce Case
content: The top court said the "feudalistic" approach of a Gujarat family court in holding a woman's pursuit of her professional career as a dentist as "cruelty" and "desertion" was "regressive" and "ultra-conservative".

title: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test LIVE Updates: TVK's Win, AIADMK vs AIADMK, Astrologer Pick Row In Assembly
content: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test LIVE Updates: A rebel faction of the AIADMK, comprising 30 MLAs, said they will support Vijay in Wednesday's floor test.

title: India Loses $18.3 Billion On Edible Oil Imports. Why Don't We Produce It?
content: India imports 15-16 million tonnes ofedible oil annually because farmers see oilseeds as "risky" crops.

title: Prateek Yadav Death News Updates: Mulayam Singh Yadav's Son Prateek Yadav Dies At 38 After Lung Illness
content: Prateek Yadav Death Updates: Prateek Yadav was taken to Lucknow's Civil Hospital, where he was declared brought dead.

title: Bihar Woman, 23, Killed Four Days After Wedding Over Gold Chain, Body Burnt
content: During the four days that Sanju and Vinod's marriage lasted before the bride was killed, she was allegedly physically and mentally assaulted.

title: New Labour Code Opens A Door To 4-Day Work Week, But Conditions Apply
content: Code on Wages (Central) Rules: Under the new provision, the condensed work week typically could mean 12-hour workdays for four days.

title: China Draws Four 'Red-Lines' In Ties With US Ahead Of Trump-Xi Summit
content: China views the democratically governed Taiwan as its own territory. Therefore, the United States' weapons sales to Taipei is certain to be discussed during the two days of meetings this week between Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping.

title: 3 Church Leaders Shot Dead In Manipur's Kangpokpi
content: The victims were travelling in two vehicles and were returning to Kangpokpi from Churachandpur district after attending a TBA Conference in Lamka when they were attacked midway.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Man Who Split AIADMK: Edappadi Palaniswami And His Politics Of Self-Sabotage
content: Many in the party are no longer willing to put up with repeated failures the AIADMK has faced under Palaniswami.
